task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution correctly calculates the trimmed mean, handles edge cases, but could be more concise.
trace: I approached this task by first handling the edge case where the input list is empty or contains less than three elements, as per the specified criteria. I then sorted the list and removed exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum using their indices. The sum of the remaining numbers was calculated and finally, the trimmed mean was returned as a float by dividing the total with the new length of the list minus 2 (the number of discarded elements).
